title: House Passes Iran War Powers Resolution in Bipartisan Rebuke to Trump
summary: A measure to direct an end to U.S. engagement in Iran was adopted with a handful of Republicans in support, sending a signal of opposition to the president’s handling of the war.

title: Republicans Begin to Test the Limits of Trump’s Power by Flexing Their Own
summary: The president’s unilateral and retributive style of governing is starting to hit a wall in both chambers of Congress.

title: Massie, 3 Other House Republicans Broke From Trump on Iran War Powers Vote
summary: Four Republicans from different ideological factions crossed party lines to vote with Democrats in favor of reining in the president’s power to wage war unilaterally.

title: House Advances New Sanctions on Russia and Aid to Ukraine
summary: After G.O.P. leaders blocked additional aid to Ukraine, six Republicans and an independent joined Democrats to force the measure to the floor against the wishes of the speaker.

title: G.O.P. Revives Immigration Bill, Weighing Ban on Trump’s Fund
summary: Several Republicans suggested they would insist on adding a measure to bar the president from creating a fund to pay people who claim to be victims of government persecution.

title: ‘I Love It’: Trump Is Still in Favor of $1.8 Billion Payout Fund
summary: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, said on Tuesday that the administration was “not moving forward with the fund, period,” after the plan drew bipartisan backlash.

title: ‘Infuriated’ Former Judges Take on Trump
summary: Critics say it is unseemly for retired judges to trade on the prestige of their former positions.

title: Park Service Awards No-Bid Contract to Cover Bridge Statues in Gold
summary: Originally estimated to cost $2.4 million, the government will now spend $5 million to restore the bronzes in time for Independence Day.

title: We Interrupt This Knicks Victory for a Political Ad Starring Mamdani
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani lent his political star power to an unusual TV ad promoting three Democrats running for House seats in New York City.

title: SpaceX IPO to Be Largest Ever at $135 Share Price
summary: The $135 share price means Elon Musk’s rocket maker is poised to exceed the 2019 initial public offering of Saudi Aramco in both valuation and money raised.

title: Amid War, Iran’s Soccer Leader Works to Get His Team to the World Cup
summary: Mehdi Taj said his federation has been dealing with FIFA, not the United States, in trying to obtain visas for Iran to play in the tournament that begins next week.

title: Bari Weiss Speaks on Scott Pelley’s ’60 Minutes’ Firing: ‘That’s the Path That He Chose’
summary: In her first public comments on the firing, Ms. Weiss, the CBS News editor in chief, said that the longtime correspondent had “broken” the trust in the newsroom.

title: In Alabama Ruling, Supreme Court Signals Limited Role for Federal Courts in Redistricting Fights
summary: In an emergency ruling on Tuesday night, the court’s conservative majority gave a first glimpse into congressional district battles under a weakened Voting Rights Act.

title: With His New Museum, Obama Offers a Trip to a Parallel America
summary: The presidential center that opens this month seems out of step with the zeitgeist of the moment and produces emotional reactions among the discontented of the Trump era.

title: Sherrill Says Immigration Officials Won’t Let Her Visit Detention Center
summary: The governor said the Department of Homeland Security’s actions at Delaney Hall raise “serious questions.”

title: Louisiana ICE Facility Mistreated Immigrants, Federal Investigators Say
summary: A report by the Department of Homeland Security’s internal watchdog described officers putting one man in a chokehold and stabbing another with a pen.

title: Four Immigrant Workers Burned to Death in Italy
summary: The immigrants had been working as fruit pickers in southern Italy, the authorities said, apparently as part of a system of criminal labor trafficking.

title: Pace Gallery Cuts 50 Artists and 50 Staff Amid Art Market Challenges
summary: A challenging art market is forcing even an established player to contract.

title: Melinda French Gates: America Needs a Menopause Revolution
summary: Too many women walk out of their doctors’ office with no diagnosis, no treatment and no plan.

title: Grow, Hair, Grow
summary: You spend your whole childhood trying to look different from your dad, only to wake up and see his forehead in the mirror.

title: Late Night Reacts to Another Reality Star Wooing Voters
summary: Michael Kosta wasn’t impressed to see that the L.A. mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt “got a second-season pickup” after Tuesday’s midterms.

title: Flesh-Eating Pest Confirmed in U.S. Cattle
summary: The New World screwworm was found in a calf in South Texas, the Agriculture Department said, the first case in the country since the 1960s.
